surface_verification.justification: Framework CI-policy change: the diff-coverage gate flips from warn-only to hard-blocking (drop continue-on-error + remove the ci_gate_allowlist entry). No target-app runtime surface. The consistency of the flipped ci.yml + removed allowlist entry is verified end-to-end by the live-repo guard (test_check_ci_gate_coverage_io.py::TestRealRepo) which recomputes the guard against the real .github/workflows.

behaviors.behavior: ci.yml 'Diff coverage (gate)' step has NO continue-on-error (hard gate) yet still invokes the tested --fail-under 80 wrapper with pinned diff-cover
no ci_gate_allowlist entry remains for the diff-coverage step (the flip removed it)
the LIVE workflows still pass the CI-gate guard with the now-non-loose step and no stale allowlist entry
a continue-on-error diff-coverage gate step WOULD be flagged loose (regression guard against silently re-adding warn-only)
Combine coverage still runs before the renamed 'Diff coverage (gate)' step
the gate decision itself (fail on <80, pass otherwise, fail-closed on crash) is unchanged and still correct
behaviors.evidence: shared/scripts/tools/tests/test_measure_diff_coverage_gate.py (11) + test_real_pr_gate_replay.py (20) still green after the flip

behaviors.behavior: GitHub actually blocks a merge when the hard gate's required check goes red
behaviors.reason: requires a live GitHub Actions runner + branch-protection required-check enforcement; cannot be reproduced locally.
